title: Route Alerts in Alertmanager
description:
## Send Alerts Somewhere

Alertmanager config has a top-level **`route`** that selects a **`receiver`**, and a **`receivers`** list defining those receivers.

**Your task:** write **`/root/alertmanager.yml`** with:
1. a **`route`** whose **`receiver`** is **`team-pager`**
2. a **`receivers`** list containing an entry whose **`name`** is **`team-pager`**

> 💡 The route's receiver name must match a defined receiver. Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/alertmanager.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Route + matching receiver
hints.body: Set route.receiver, then declare a receiver with the same name in the receivers list.
hints.command:
printf 'route:
  receiver: team-pager
  group_by: ["alertname"]
receivers:
  - name: team-pager
' > /root/alertmanager.yml

validation.description: Checks the route receiver matches a defined receiver name.

commands.description: route.receiver matches a receiver named team-pager
commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/alertmanager.yml')); r=d.get('route',{}).get('receiver'); recv=[x.get('name') for x in (d.get('receivers') or [])]; print('ok' if r=='team-pager' and r in recv else 'no')"
